zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Legh於1248年出生在英國英格蘭爾希 萊時，她的父親Richard de Legh28歲，母親Mrs. Richard de Legh24歲。她於1262年與William d'Anvers III在英國英格蘭爾希 萊結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1300年在英國英格蘭爾享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Legh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Legh was born in 1248, in High Legh, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de Legh, was 28 and her mother, Mrs. Richard de Legh, was 24. She married William d'Anvers III in 1262, in High Legh, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1300, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Legh est née en 1248, à High Legh, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Legh, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Richard de Legh, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé William d'Anvers III en 1262, à High Legh, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1300, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Legh(이)가 1248년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 히 레그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Legh(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Richard de Legh(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1262년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 히 레그h에서 William d'Anvers III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Legh родилась в 1248 г. в местности хаи лег, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard de Legh было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Richard de Legh, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William d'Anvers III в 1262 г. в местности хаи лег, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1300 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет.
